rules.description: Detects the execution of StartInstance action in OCI.

rules.title: OCI Action: StopInstance
rules.description: Detects the execution of StopInstance action in OCI.

rules.title: OCI Action: TerminateInstance
rules.description: Detects the execution of TerminateInstance action in OCI.

rules.title: OCI Action: UpdateBucket
rules.description: Detects the execution of UpdateBucket action in OCI.

rules.title: OCI Action: UpdatePolicy
rules.description: Detects the execution of UpdatePolicy action in OCI.

rules.title: OCI Action: UpdateRouteTable
rules.description: Detects the execution of UpdateRouteTable action in OCI.

rules.title: OCI Action: UpdateSecurityList
rules.description: Detects the execution of UpdateSecurityList action in OCI.

rules.title: OCI API Key Uploaded
rules.description: Detects upload of a new API key to a user account.

rules.title: OCI Audit Configuration Changed
rules.description: Detects modifications to OCI Audit service configuration including retention period changes. Reducing audit retention is a defense evasion tactic.

rules.title: OCI Audit Configuration Retention Reduced
rules.description: Detects changes to audit configuration that may shorten retention period to destroy evidence.

rules.title: OCI Auth Token Created
rules.description: Detects creation of auth tokens (SWIFT passwords) which provide programmatic access to OCI Object Storage and Container Registry. Could indicate credential staging for data exfiltration.

rules.title: OCI Autonomous Database Terminated
rules.description: Detects termination of an OCI Autonomous Database, which may indicate sabotage or unauthorized resource destruction.

rules.title: OCI Bastion Session Created
rules.description: Detects creation of a Bastion session providing SSH or port-forwarding access to private resources. Unusual bastion sessions may indicate lateral movement.

rules.title: OCI Boot Volume Backup Created by Non-Admin
rules.description: Detects boot volume backup creation which could be used to copy instance data for exfiltration.

rules.title: OCI Cloud Infrastructure Discovery
rules.description: Detects excessive cloud infrastructure enumeration via OCI API calls. Attackers with compromised credentials perform reconnaissance by listing instances, networks, buckets, and databases to map the cloud environment.

rules.title: OCI Cloud Shell Session Started
rules.description: Detects OCI Cloud Shell session activation. Cloud Shell provides authenticated CLI access to the tenancy and can be used for reconnaissance, data access, or lateral movement.

rules.title: OCI Compartment Deleted
rules.description: Detects deletion of an OCI compartment. This is a high-impact destructive action that removes all resources within the compartment and could indicate sabotage or insider threat.

rules.title: OCI Compute Instance Terminated
rules.description: Detects successful termination of a compute instance, indicating potential resource destruction or evidence tampering.

rules.title: OCI Console Login Failure
rules.description: Detects failed console login attempts to OCI, which may indicate brute-force or credential stuffing attacks.

rules.title: OCI Console Login from Suspicious IP Range
rules.description: Detects console login events that may indicate unauthorized access from unusual sources.

rules.title: OCI Console Login from Unusual IP
rules.description: Detects a successful login to the OCI Console from an IP address not typically associated with the user.

rules.title: OCI Cross-Region Data Copy
rules.description: Detects cross-region copy operations for boot volumes, block volumes, or object storage buckets. This could indicate data staging for exfiltration to a region outside organizational control.

rules.title: OCI Customer Secret Key Created
rules.description: Detects creation of customer secret keys (S3-compatible access keys) for OCI Object Storage. These keys provide direct API access and may be used for data exfiltration.

rules.title: OCI Database Backup Exported
rules.description: Detects database backup creation that could be used to extract sensitive data.

rules.title: OCI Database System Terminated
rules.description: Detects termination of OCI Database Systems (BaseDB, Exadata). Unauthorized database termination can result in critical data loss and service disruption.

rules.title: OCI DRG Attachment Created
rules.description: Detects creation of Dynamic Routing Gateway attachment, enabling network connectivity between VCNs or on-premises.

rules.title: OCI Dynamic Group Created
rules.description: Detects creation of dynamic groups which grant instance principal permissions. Attackers can abuse this for lateral movement from compromised compute instances.

rules.title: OCI Dynamic Group Created with Broad Matching
rules.description: Detects creation of dynamic groups with instance principal matching that may grant excessive permissions.

rules.title: OCI Federated Identity Provider Modified
rules.description: Detects modification of SAML/OIDC identity provider configuration which could enable unauthorized access.

rules.title: OCI Function Invoked
rules.description: Detects invocation of OCI Functions (serverless). Function invocations should be monitored as they can execute arbitrary code with the function's resource principal permissions.

rules.title: OCI IAM Admin Policy Created with Manage All
rules.description: Detects creation of an IAM policy containing overly permissive 'manage all-resources' statement, which grants full administrative access.

rules.title: OCI IAM Policy Modified
rules.description: Detects changes to OCI IAM Policies.

rules.title: OCI Identity Provider Created
rules.description: Detects creation of a new identity provider (SAML/OIDC federation), which could indicate an attacker establishing persistent backdoor access via federated identity.

rules.title: OCI Instance Console Connection Created
rules.description: Detects creation of serial or VNC console connections to compute instances. Console connections bypass network controls and may indicate unauthorized access.

rules.title: OCI Instance Metadata Service Accessed
rules.description: Detects access to instance metadata service which may reveal instance principal credentials.

rules.title: OCI KMS Key Scheduled for Deletion
rules.description: Detects scheduling an OCI Vault key for deletion. This can render encrypted data permanently inaccessible.

rules.title: OCI KMS Key Version Disabled
rules.description: Detects disabling of a KMS key version which may impact data encryption or indicate key compromise response.

rules.title: OCI Local Peering Gateway Created
rules.description: Detects creation of VCN local peering gateway enabling communication between VCNs within same region.

rules.title: OCI Log Archival Policy Disabled
rules.description: Detects modification of logging configuration that may reduce log retention or disable archival.

rules.title: OCI Log Group Deleted
rules.description: Detects deletion of OCI Log Analytics log groups. Deleting log groups destroys collected log data and can indicate an attacker covering their tracks.

rules.title: OCI MFA Fatigue Attack Indicators
rules.description: Detects multiple MFA enrollment or verification attempts that may indicate MFA fatigue push attack.

rules.title: OCI Network Firewall Policy Modified
rules.description: Detects modifications to OCI Network Firewall policies. Unauthorized changes could weaken network security posture or create bypass routes.

rules.title: OCI Network Load Balancer Deleted
rules.description: Detects deletion of an OCI load balancer, which may cause service outages or indicate infrastructure sabotage.

rules.title: OCI Network Security Group Rule Added for All Protocols
rules.description: Detects adding NSG rules that allow all protocols, potentially opening attack surface.

rules.title: OCI Network Security Group Updated
rules.description: Detects updates to Network Security Groups (NSGs).

rules.title: OCI Notification Subscription Created
rules.description: Detects creation of notification topic subscriptions. Attackers may create subscriptions to exfiltrate alerts or intercept security notifications.

rules.title: OCI Object Storage Bucket Made Public
rules.description: Detects when a bucket's visibility is set to public.

rules.title: OCI Object Storage Pre-Authenticated Request Created
rules.description: Detects creation of pre-authenticated requests (PARs) for OCI Object Storage. PARs bypass IAM authentication and can be used for data exfiltration via shareable URLs.

rules.title: OCI Object Storage Replication Policy Created
rules.description: Detects creation of cross-region object storage replication policy, a potential data movement vector.

rules.title: OCI Password Spraying Attack
rules.description: Detects password spraying by monitoring failed OCI Console login attempts across multiple user accounts. Unlike brute force (many attempts against one user), password spraying tries a few passwords against many accounts to avoid lockout thresholds.

rules.title: OCI Policy Allows Manage All Resources
rules.description: Detects update of existing IAM policies to include manage all-resources, an overprivileged access pattern that may indicate privilege escalation.

rules.title: OCI Route Table Update
rules.description: Detects changes to VCN Route Tables.

rules.title: OCI Security List Allows All Protocols
rules.description: Detects security list updates that allow all protocols (protocol=all) from any source, creating an overly permissive network configuration that violates STIG network segmentation requirements.

rules.title: OCI Service Gateway Created
rules.description: Detects creation of a service gateway that bypasses internet to access OCI services, a potential lateral movement path.

rules.title: OCI User Capabilities Escalation
rules.description: Detects enabling console password for a user, potentially escalating a service account to interactive access.

rules.title: OCI User MFA Not Enabled
rules.description: Detects OCI user accounts where multi-factor authentication has been disabled or not configured, violating STIG requirement for strong authentication.

rules.title: OCI User Password Reset by Admin
rules.description: Detects an administrator resetting another user's console password. This could indicate account takeover or unauthorized access provisioning.

rules.title: OCI Vault Key Rotation Overdue
rules.description: Detects disabling of key rotation schedule for OCI Vault encryption keys. Key rotation is a STIG requirement for cryptographic key management.

rules.title: OCI Vault Secret Deleted
rules.description: Detects deletion of secrets from OCI Vault. Secret deletion can indicate an attacker covering tracks or disrupting application functionality.

rules.title: OCI Vault Secret Version Deprecated
rules.description: Detects deprecation of vault secret versions which may indicate secret rotation bypass.

rules.title: OCI VCN Flow Log Disabled
rules.description: Detects deletion or disabling of VCN flow logs which may indicate an attempt to hide network activity.

rules.title: OCI VCN Peering Connection Created
rules.description: Detects creation of VCN peering connections (local or remote). Unauthorized peering can extend network access across compartments or regions, enabling lateral movement.

rules.title: OCI VCN Security List Open to World
rules.description: Detects creation or update of a Security List allowing ingress from 0.0.0.0/0.

rules.title: OCI WAF Configuration Updated
rules.description: Detects changes to Web Application Firewall configurations.

rules.title: OCI WAF Policy Deleted
rules.description: Detects deletion of Web Application Firewall policies which may expose applications to attacks.

rules.title: Office Application Startup Persistence
rules.description: Detects modification of Office startup locations for add-in persistence.

rules.title: Parent PID Spoofing
rules.description: Detects indicators of Parent PID spoofing, where a process fakes its parent for evasion.

rules.title: Pass-the-Ticket via Rubeus
rules.description: Detects Rubeus commands for pass-the-ticket lateral movement using Kerberos tickets.

rules.title: Pass-the-Ticket: Excessive Explicit Credential Logons
rules.description: Detects Event 4648 explicit credential delegation at high frequency from a single source — indicates pass-the-ticket, over-pass-the-hash, or Rubeus asktgt lateral movement across the domain.

rules.title: Password Policy Discovery
rules.description: Detects commands querying password policy settings, often part of pre-brute-force recon.

rules.title: Port Monitor DLL Persistence
rules.description: Detects registration of port monitor DLLs which run in the context of the Print Spooler service.

rules.title: Potato Privilege Escalation Tool
rules.description: Detects execution of various Potato family privilege escalation tools (JuicyPotato, SweetPotato, etc.).

rules.title: PowerShell Execution via Alternate Shell
rules.description: Detects PowerShell execution using alternate host processes (pwsh, powershell_ise) that may bypass logging.

rules.title: PowerShell Script Block with Suspicious Keywords
rules.description: Detects PowerShell commands containing keywords commonly associated with offensive techniques.

rules.title: PowerShell: Suspicious Command Execution (Real Windows Security Events)
rules.description: Detects suspicious PowerShell commands in Windows Security Event 4688 process creation logs. Covers download cradles, encoded execution, AMSI bypass, credential access, and common attack framework commands. Optimized for real Windows Security Events (not Sysmon) using Original Log Content matching.

rules.title: Print Processor Persistence
rules.description: Detects registration of malicious print processors for persistence via the Print Spooler service.

rules.title: PrintNightmare Exploitation Attempt
rules.description: Detects exploitation of Print Spooler vulnerability (CVE-2021-34527) for privilege escalation.

rules.title: Privilege Escalation: Sensitive Privileges Assigned to Non-Admin
rules.description: Detects Event 4672 with dangerous privileges (SeDebugPrivilege, SeTcbPrivilege) assigned to non-SYSTEM accounts. Indicates token manipulation or privilege escalation.

rules.title: Process Discovery via Tasklist
rules.description: Detects tasklist.exe with verbose or SVC flags often used during post-exploitation reconnaissance.

rules.title: Process Doppelganging via TxF
rules.description: Detects indicators of process doppelganging using transactional NTFS to replace process memory.

rules.title: Process Ghosting or Herpaderping
rules.description: Detects indicators of process ghosting or herpaderping evasion techniques based on suspicious process creation patterns.

rules.title: Process Injection via CreateRemoteThread
rules.description: Detects suspicious processes commonly used as injection vehicles with command-line indicators of injection.

rules.title: PsExec Service Installation
rules.description: Detects PsExec creating its service (PSEXESVC) on a remote machine.

rules.title: Python Execution as Child of System Process
rules.description: Detects Python being executed as a child of system services or common LOLBins.

rules.title: Query Registry for Security Products
rules.description: Detects registry queries checking for installed security products.

rules.title: Ransomware File Extension Indicators
rules.description: Detects commands associated with ransomware encryption activities based on known file extension patterns.

rules.title: RDP Session Hijacking via tscon
rules.description: Detects RDP session hijacking using tscon.exe to connect to disconnected sessions.

rules.title: Reflective DLL Loading Indicators
rules.description: Detects indicators of reflective DLL loading where DLLs are loaded from memory without touching disk.

rules.title: Registry Run Key Modification via Reg.exe
rules.description: Detects registry run key modifications using reg.exe for persistence.

rules.title: Regsvcs or Regasm Execution for Code Bypass
rules.description: Detects execution of Regsvcs.exe or Regasm.exe to register COM objects from untrusted locations.

rules.title: Remote Service Creation via SC
rules.description: Detects remote service creation using sc.exe with the \\hostname syntax.

rules.title: Renamed System Binary Execution
rules.description: Detects execution of renamed system utilities where the original filename does not match the current image name.

rules.title: Root Certificate Installation via Certutil
rules.description: Detects installation of root certificates which could enable MitM attacks or trust arbitrary code.

rules.title: SAM Database Extraction via Reg Save
rules.description: Detects extraction of the SAM database using reg.exe save command, used to obtain password hashes offline.

rules.title: Scheduled Task XML Import
rules.description: Detects importing scheduled tasks from XML files, which may bypass task creation monitoring.

rules.title: Screen Capture via PowerShell
rules.description: Detects PowerShell commands capturing screenshots using .NET classes.

rules.title: ScreenSaver Hijacking Persistence
rules.description: Detects modification of screensaver registry settings for persistence.

rules.title: Scripting Engine Spawning Network Utility
rules.description: Detects scripting engines spawning network utilities like curl, wget, or net.exe.

rules.title: SDelete Secure File Deletion
rules.description: Detects usage of Sysinternals SDelete for secure file deletion to destroy forensic evidence.

rules.title: Security Group Enumeration: Rapid Membership Queries
rules.description: Detects Event 4799 rapid security group membership enumeration — indicates BloodHound/SharpHound or net group /domain reconnaissance.

rules.title: Security Support Provider DLL Persistence
rules.description: Detects registration of Security Support Provider DLLs for credential interception.

rules.title: SeDebugPrivilege Abuse
rules.description: Detects tools attempting to enable or abuse SeDebugPrivilege for process manipulation.

rules.title: Sensitive Data Endpoint Access
rules.description: Detects access to endpoints that may expose sensitive data (database exports, configuration files, backup files). OWASP A02:2021 Cryptographic Failures / A01 Broken Access Control.

rules.title: Service Execution via sc.exe Create
rules.description: Detects creation of new Windows services via sc.exe, which can be used for persistence or privilege escalation.

rules.title: Service Permissions Weakness Discovery
rules.description: Detects tools checking for weak service permissions that allow privilege escalation.

rules.title: Service Stop via Net Stop
rules.description: Detects stopping of critical services that may indicate pre-ransomware activity.

rules.title: Shadow Credentials Attack via Whisker
rules.description: Detects Shadow Credentials attacks using Whisker or similar tools that modify msDS-KeyCredentialLink attribute.

rules.title: SharpRDP Lateral Movement
rules.description: Detects indicators of SharpRDP or similar tools performing authenticated RDP lateral movement.

rules.title: Shortcut Modification for Persistence
rules.description: Detects creation of suspicious .lnk shortcut files using PowerShell COM objects.

rules.title: Software Discovery via WMIC
rules.description: Detects software enumeration using WMIC to identify installed applications.

rules.title: SSRF to Cloud Instance Metadata Service (Linux)
rules.description:
Detects outbound HTTP requests to the cloud instance metadata endpoint
(169.254.169.254) from web application processes on Linux. This indicates
Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF) exploitation targeting OCI/AWS/GCP/Azure
IMDS to extract instance credentials or configuration.
Covers MITRE ATT&CK T1552.005 (Cloud Instance Metadata API).

rules.title: SSRF to Cloud Metadata Endpoint (169.254.169.254)
rules.description:
Detects outbound network connections to the cloud instance metadata service
(169.254.169.254) from web application or scripting processes. This indicates
Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF) where an attacker exploits a web app to
access the OCI/AWS/GCP/Azure IMDS endpoint and steal credentials, instance
identity tokens, or configuration data.
Covers MITRE ATT&CK T1552.005 (Cloud Instance Metadata API).

rules.title: Startup Folder Modification
rules.description: Detects commands copying or creating files in the Windows startup folder.

rules.title: Suspicious HTTP Method Usage
rules.description: Detects use of unusual or dangerous HTTP methods (PUT, DELETE, TRACE, OPTIONS, PATCH) that may indicate API abuse or security misconfiguration. OWASP A05:2021 Security Misconfiguration.

rules.title: Suspicious or Empty User Agent Detected
rules.description: Detects requests with empty, missing, or known-malicious user agents. Automated attack tools often use non-standard user agents or omit them entirely.

rules.title: Suspicious Scheduled Task Creation
rules.description: Detects schtasks.exe creating tasks that execute scripts or binaries from user-writable paths.

rules.title: Suspicious Usage of base64
rules.description: Detects execution of base64 with decode or sensitive file operations.

rules.title: Suspicious Usage of chmod
rules.description: Detects execution of chmod with SUID/world-writable permission changes.

rules.title: Suspicious Usage of chown
rules.description: Detects execution of chown targeting system directories or changing to root ownership.

rules.title: Suspicious Usage of curl
rules.description: Detects execution of curl downloading to temp directories or piping to shell.

rules.title: Suspicious Usage of dd
rules.description: Detects execution of dd accessing raw devices or memory.

rules.title: Suspicious Usage of gdb
rules.description: Detects execution of gdb attaching to process or dumping memory.

rules.title: Suspicious Usage of id
rules.description: Detects execution of id in command chain suggesting enumeration.

rules.title: Suspicious Usage of insmod
rules.description: Detects execution of insmod loading modules from non-standard paths.

rules.title: Suspicious Usage of lua
rules.description: Detects execution of lua with command execution capabilities.

rules.title: Suspicious Usage of modprobe
rules.description: Detects execution of modprobe loading modules from non-standard paths.

rules.title: Suspicious Usage of nc
rules.description: Detects execution of nc with listen or execute flags.

rules.title: Suspicious Usage of ncat
rules.description: Detects execution of ncat with execute or listen flags.

rules.title: Suspicious Usage of netcat
rules.description: Detects execution of netcat with execute or listen flags.

rules.title: Suspicious Usage of nmap
rules.description: Detects execution of nmap with scanning or fingerprinting flags.

rules.title: Suspicious Usage of passwd
rules.description: Detects execution of passwd with automated password change or removal.

rules.title: Suspicious Usage of perl
rules.description: Detects execution of perl with inline execution or network operations.

rules.title: Suspicious Usage of python
rules.description: Detects execution of python with inline execution or suspicious imports.

rules.title: Suspicious Usage of rmmod
rules.description: Detects execution of rmmod removing kernel modules (potential rootkit management).

rules.title: Suspicious Usage of ruby
rules.description: Detects execution of ruby with inline execution or network operations.

rules.title: Suspicious Usage of shadow
rules.description: Detects execution of shadow with direct access to shadow password file.

rules.title: Suspicious Usage of socat
rules.description: Detects execution of socat with network or execution redirections.

rules.title: Suspicious Usage of strace
rules.description: Detects execution of strace tracing other processes.

rules.title: Suspicious Usage of tcpdump
rules.description: Detects execution of tcpdump capturing network traffic.

rules.title: Suspicious Usage of tshark
rules.description: Detects execution of tshark capturing network traffic.

rules.title: Suspicious Usage of wget
rules.description: Detects execution of wget downloading to temp directories or bypassing cert checks.

rules.title: Suspicious Usage of whoami
rules.description: Detects execution of whoami in command chain suggesting post-exploitation enumeration.

rules.title: Suspicious Usage of wireshark
rules.description: Detects execution of wireshark potentially capturing network traffic.

rules.title: SyncAppvPublishingServer Abuse
rules.description: Detects abuse of SyncAppvPublishingServer.exe for code execution through PowerShell.

rules.title: Sysmon C2 Beacon - Periodic Outbound HTTPS
rules.description: Detects periodic outbound HTTPS connections to non-standard destinations that may indicate C2 beaconing activity. Suspicious processes making repeated connections to external IPs on port 443 are flagged.

rules.title: Sysmon Cobalt Strike C2 Network Indicators
rules.description: Detects network connections from processes commonly associated with Cobalt Strike beacon activity. Includes default Cobalt Strike process injection targets and malleable C2 patterns.

rules.title: Sysmon Cobalt Strike Named Pipe
rules.description: Detects named pipe creation matching known Cobalt Strike default patterns. Cobalt Strike uses named pipes for inter-process communication and lateral movement via SMB beacon.

rules.title: Sysmon Configuration Tampering
rules.description: Detects attempts to unload or reconfigure Sysmon to blind endpoint monitoring.

rules.title: Sysmon DNS Data Exfiltration
rules.description: Detects DNS queries with high-entropy subdomains that may indicate data exfiltration via DNS tunneling. Encoded data in subdomain labels is a common technique for covert data transfer.

rules.title: Sysmon DNS Query to Known C2 Framework Domains
rules.description: Detects DNS queries to domains associated with known C2 frameworks and dynamic DNS services commonly used by attackers for C2 infrastructure.

rules.title: Sysmon DNS Query to Suspicious TLDs
rules.description: Detects DNS queries to top-level domains frequently associated with malicious infrastructure, phishing, and C2 operations.

rules.title: Sysmon DNS Tunneling via Network Connection
rules.description: Detects suspicious processes making DNS connections (port 53) that could indicate DNS tunneling for data exfiltration or C2 communication.

rules.title: Sysmon Kerberoasting Network Indicator
rules.description: Detects Kerberos ticket requests (port 88) from anomalous processes. Kerberoasting attacks request service tickets (TGS) to crack service account passwords offline.

rules.title: Sysmon Lateral Movement via SMB
rules.description: Detects SMB connections (port 445) from processes commonly used for lateral movement. Attackers use PsExec, WMI, and other tools to move laterally through SMB/Windows Admin Shares.

rules.title: Sysmon Lateral Movement via WinRM
rules.description: Detects WinRM/PSRemoting connections (ports 5985/5986) from suspicious processes. Attackers use PowerShell Remoting for lateral movement and remote command execution.

rules.title: Sysmon LDAP Reconnaissance
rules.description: Detects LDAP enumeration patterns (port 389/636) from suspicious processes. Attackers use LDAP queries to enumerate Active Directory users, groups, computers, and service accounts.

rules.title: Sysmon Mimikatz Named Pipe
rules.description: Detects named pipe creation matching known Mimikatz pipe patterns. Mimikatz uses named pipes for credential extraction and token manipulation operations.

rules.title: Sysmon Mimikatz Network Activity
rules.description: Detects network connections from mimikatz or known credential theft tools accessing domain controller services (LDAP, Kerberos, SMB).

rules.title: Sysmon PsExec Named Pipe
rules.description: Detects named pipe creation matching PsExec default pipe names. PsExec creates named pipes for remote command execution and is commonly used for lateral movement.

rules.title: Sysmon RDP Lateral Movement
rules.description: Detects outbound RDP connections (port 3389) from unusual processes. While RDP is commonly used for administration, connections from non-standard processes may indicate lateral movement.

rules.title: Sysmon Suspicious Named Pipe Pattern
rules.description: Detects named pipe creation matching common C2 framework patterns including Metasploit, Covenant, and other post-exploitation tools.

rules.title: Sysmon Suspicious Outbound Connection from LOLBin
rules.description: Detects outbound network connections from Living-off-the-Land binaries (LOLBins) to external IP addresses. These binaries are commonly abused by attackers for downloading payloads and establishing C2.

rules.title: System Recovery Disabled via BCDEdit
rules.description: Detects BCDEdit commands disabling system recovery, commonly seen in ransomware attacks.

rules.title: System Shutdown or Reboot via shutdown.exe
rules.description: Detects forced system shutdown or reboot commands that may be part of destructive operations.

rules.title: Template Injection via Microsoft Office
rules.description: Detects Office applications loading remote templates, a technique to deliver malicious macros.

rules.title: Time Provider DLL Persistence
rules.description: Detects registration of time provider DLLs for high-privilege persistence.

rules.title: Timestomping via PowerShell
rules.description: Detects PowerShell commands modifying file timestamps to blend malicious files with legitimate ones.

rules.title: Token Impersonation via Incognito
rules.description: Detects usage of Incognito or similar tools for Windows access token manipulation and impersonation.

rules.title: Token Manipulation via RunAs
rules.description: Detects suspicious RunAs usage that may indicate token manipulation for privilege escalation.

rules.title: UAC Bypass via ComputerDefaults
rules.description: Detects UAC bypass using computerdefaults.exe auto-elevation.

rules.title: UAC Bypass via DiskCleanup
rules.description: Detects UAC bypass using environment variable manipulation with DiskCleanup.

rules.title: UAC Bypass via Eventvwr
rules.description: Detects UAC bypass using eventvwr.exe registry hijacking.

rules.title: UAC Bypass via Fodhelper
rules.description: Detects UAC bypass using fodhelper.exe to execute commands with elevated privileges.

rules.title: Unquoted Service Path Exploitation
rules.description: Detects commands querying for unquoted service paths, a privilege escalation vector.

rules.title: Unusually Large HTTP Response (Data Exfiltration)
rules.description: Detects unusually large HTTP responses that may indicate successful data exfiltration (database dumps, bulk user data extraction). OWASP A01:2021 Broken Access Control.

rules.title: VBA Macro Spawning Suspicious Child Process
rules.description: Detects Office applications spawning command shells or scripting engines, indicating macro execution.

rules.title: Virtualization Sandbox Evasion Check
rules.description: Detects commands checking for virtualization or sandbox environments before executing payloads.

rules.title: Visual Basic Script Compilation via vbc.exe
rules.description: Detects compilation of Visual Basic code using vbc.exe from non-standard locations.

rules.title: Volume Shadow Copy Deletion via WMIC
rules.description: Detects deletion of volume shadow copies using WMIC, a common ransomware preparation step.

rules.title: WAF Command Injection Attack Blocked
rules.description: Detects OS command injection attempts blocked by OCI WAF. Attackers inject system commands through web parameters. OWASP A03:2021 Injection.

rules.title: WAF CORS Bypass Attempt Blocked
rules.description: Detects CORS misconfiguration exploitation attempts where attackers try to bypass cross-origin restrictions. OWASP A05:2021 Security Misconfiguration.

rules.title: WAF Cross-Site Scripting Attack Blocked
rules.description: Detects XSS (Cross-Site Scripting) attempts blocked by OCI WAF. Part of OWASP A03:2021 Injection category.

rules.title: WAF LDAP Injection Attack Blocked
rules.description: Detects LDAP injection attempts blocked by WAF. Attackers manipulate LDAP queries to bypass authentication or extract directory information. OWASP A03:2021 Injection.

rules.title: WAF Log4Shell (CVE-2021-44228) Attack Blocked
rules.description: Detects Log4Shell (Log4j RCE) exploitation attempts blocked by WAF. Attackers use JNDI lookup strings to achieve remote code execution. OWASP A06:2021 Vulnerable and Outdated Components.

rules.title: WAF NoSQL Injection Attack Blocked
rules.description: Detects NoSQL injection attempts (MongoDB, CouchDB) blocked by WAF. Attackers inject NoSQL query operators to bypass authentication or extract data. OWASP A03:2021 Injection.

rules.title: WAF Path Traversal Attack Blocked
rules.description: Detects directory traversal (path traversal) attacks blocked by WAF. Attackers attempt to access files outside the web root using ../ sequences. OWASP A01:2021 Broken Access Control.

rules.title: WAF Protocol Attack Blocked
rules.description: Detects HTTP protocol violation attacks blocked by WAF (request smuggling, header injection, HTTP splitting). OWASP A05:2021 Security Misconfiguration.

rules.title: WAF Rate Limiting Triggered
rules.description: Detects WAF rate limiting events indicating automated attack tools or brute force attempts. Rate limiting is configured at 100 requests per minute for Seven Kingdoms Portal.

rules.title: WAF Server-Side Request Forgery Blocked
rules.description: Detects SSRF attempts blocked by WAF where attackers try to make the server fetch internal resources. OWASP A10:2021 Server-Side Request Forgery.

rules.title: WAF Server-Side Template Injection Blocked
rules.description: Detects Server-Side Template Injection (SSTI) attempts blocked by WAF. Attackers inject template syntax to achieve remote code execution via template engines (Jinja2, Twig, Freemarker).

rules.title: WAF SQL Injection Attack Allowed Through
rules.description: Detects SQL injection attempts that were ALLOWED through WAF (detection-only mode or rule bypass). Critical alert indicating potential successful exploitation.

rules.title: WAF SQL Injection Attack Blocked
rules.description: Detects SQL injection attempts blocked by OCI WAF. Indicates active exploitation of OWASP A03:2021 Injection vulnerabilities against the web application.

rules.title: WAF Web Shell Upload Attempt Blocked
rules.description: Detects attempts to upload web shell files blocked by WAF. Web shells provide persistent remote access through the web server. OWASP A03:2021 Injection.

rules.title: WAF XML External Entity Attack Blocked
rules.description: Detects XXE (XML External Entity) injection attempts blocked by WAF. Attackers exploit XML parsers to read files, perform SSRF, or execute denial of service.

rules.title: Web Application Authentication Bypass
rules.description: Detects authentication bypass attempts including JWT manipulation, session fixation, and forced browsing to protected endpoints. OWASP A07:2021 Identification and Authentication Failures.

rules.title: Web Application Brute Force Login Attempt
rules.description: Detects repeated failed login attempts via HTTP 401/403 responses to authentication endpoints. Indicates brute force or credential stuffing attacks. OWASP A07:2021 Identification and Authentication Failures.

rules.title: Web Application Privilege Escalation
rules.description: Detects privilege escalation attempts where users try to modify their role or access admin-only endpoints. OWASP A01:2021 Broken Access Control.

rules.title: Web Application Server Error Spike
rules.description: Detects HTTP 500 Internal Server Error responses which may indicate successful injection attacks causing application errors, or application instability from attack traffic.

rules.title: Web Application Session Hijacking Indicators
rules.description: Detects indicators of session hijacking or session fixation attacks where an attacker steals or manipulates session tokens. OWASP A07:2021 Identification and Authentication Failures.

rules.title: Web Directory Enumeration Detected
rules.description: Detects directory and file enumeration attempts via high volume of 404 responses to common admin/config paths. OWASP A01:2021 Broken Access Control.

rules.title: Web Server Process Spawning Command Shell
rules.description:
Detects a web server or scripting engine process spawning a command shell
(cmd.exe, powershell.exe, bash) with suspicious command-line arguments
containing pipe, semicolon, or other command injection characters.
This indicates successful Remote Code Execution (RCE) via the web application
(e.g., OS command injection in a FastAPI/Django/Express endpoint).
Covers MITRE ATT&CK T1059 (Command and Scripting Interpreter).

rules.title: Web Server Process Spawning Shell with Injection Characters (Linux)
rules.description:
Detects a web application runtime (Python, Node, Java, PHP) spawning a shell
process (bash, sh, dash) with command-line arguments containing injection
characters (semicolons, pipes, backticks). This indicates successful Remote
Code Execution (RCE) via a command injection vulnerability in the web app.
Covers MITRE ATT&CK T1059 (Command and Scripting Interpreter) and
T1190 (Exploit Public-Facing Application).

rules.title: Web Vulnerability Scanner Detected
rules.description: Detects known web vulnerability scanner user agents in Load Balancer access logs. Indicates active reconnaissance against the web application.

rules.title: WiFi Password Extraction via Netsh
rules.description: Detects extraction of stored WiFi passwords using netsh wlan show profile with key=clear.

rules.title: Windows Access Token Manipulation
rules.description: Detects access token manipulation techniques used for privilege escalation. Attackers steal or forge tokens to impersonate other users, especially SYSTEM or domain admin, without needing their credentials.

rules.title: Windows Account Discovery Commands
rules.description: Detects commands used to enumerate user accounts and groups in Active Directory. Attackers perform account discovery to identify privileged accounts, service accounts, and domain administrators for targeting.

rules.title: Windows Admin Share Access via Net Use
rules.description: Detects access to admin shares (C$, ADMIN$, IPC$) for lateral movement.

rules.title: Windows AMSI Bypass Attempt
rules.description: Detects attempts to bypass the Antimalware Scan Interface (AMSI) through PowerShell commands or .NET reflection techniques.

rules.title: Windows Backup Deletion via wbadmin
rules.description: Detects deletion of Windows backup catalog using wbadmin, preventing backup-based recovery.

rules.title: Windows BITS Job Abuse for Persistence
rules.description: Detects abuse of Background Intelligent Transfer Service (BITS) for persistence or payload download. BITS jobs survive reboots and can notify on completion with arbitrary commands.

rules.title: Windows Boot Configuration Modified
rules.description: Detects use of bcdedit.exe to disable recovery mode or modify boot configuration, commonly seen in ransomware attacks to prevent system recovery.

rules.title: Windows Certutil Download or Decode
rules.description: Detects usage of certutil.exe to download files from URLs or decode Base64 payloads, a common LOLBin abuse technique.

rules.title: Windows Credential Dumping via Procdump
rules.description: Detects credential dumping attempts targeting the LSASS process using procdump or similar tools.

rules.title: Windows Credential Dumping via Secretsdump
rules.description: Detects execution of secretsdump.py or similar Impacket tools for credential extraction.

rules.title: Windows Credential Manager Access via VaultCmd
rules.description: Detects usage of VaultCmd.exe to enumerate or export Windows Credential Manager entries.

rules.title: Windows Data Staging for Exfiltration
rules.description: Detects data staging activity where an attacker collects and stages files before exfiltration. Indicators include copying files to temp directories, creating archives, or writing to removable media staging areas.

rules.title: Windows Defender Exclusion Added via PowerShell
rules.description: Detects addition of Windows Defender exclusions via PowerShell, often used to whitelist malware locations.

rules.title: Windows Defender Real-Time Protection Disabled
rules.description: Detects commands disabling Windows Defender real-time protection via PowerShell or registry.

rules.title: Windows DLL Side-Loading via Suspicious Path
rules.description: Detects execution of legitimate Windows binaries from non-standard paths, indicating potential DLL side-loading attacks where malicious DLLs are loaded alongside trusted executables.

rules.title: Windows Encoded PowerShell Execution
rules.description: Detects execution of PowerShell with encoded commands, commonly used to obfuscate malicious scripts.

rules.title: Windows Event Log Cleared via Wevtutil
rules.description: Detects clearing of Windows Event Logs using wevtutil.exe to remove forensic evidence.

rules.title: Windows Event Log Clearing
rules.description: Detects clearing of Windows event logs via wevtutil or PowerShell. Attackers clear logs to destroy evidence of their activities and hinder forensic investigation.

rules.title: Windows Firewall Rule Modification
rules.description: Detects modification of Windows Firewall rules via netsh. Attackers disable or modify firewall rules to enable lateral movement or C2 communication.

rules.title: Windows Kerberoasting Attack
rules.description: Detects Kerberoasting techniques using tools or PowerShell commands that request TGS tickets for service accounts to crack offline.

rules.title: Windows Keylogger Indicators
rules.description: Detects indicators of keylogging activity. Attackers deploy keyloggers to capture credentials, sensitive data, and user activity. Detects common keylogger tools, SetWindowsHookEx API calls, and suspicious input recording patterns.

rules.title: Windows LOLBin Usage: at
rules.description: Detects usage of Living off the Land binary at.

rules.title: Windows LOLBin Usage: bitsadmin
rules.description: Detects usage of Living off the Land binary bitsadmin.

rules.title: Windows LOLBin Usage: certutil
rules.description: Detects usage of Living off the Land binary certutil.

rules.title: Windows LOLBin Usage: cmd
rules.description: Detects usage of Living off the Land binary cmd.

rules.title: Windows LOLBin Usage: cscript
rules.description: Detects usage of Living off the Land binary cscript.

rules.title: Windows LOLBin Usage: ipconfig
rules.description: Detects usage of Living off the Land binary ipconfig.

rules.title: Windows LOLBin Usage: mshta
rules.description: Detects usage of Living off the Land binary mshta.

rules.title: Windows LOLBin Usage: net
rules.description: Detects usage of Living off the Land binary net.

rules.title: Windows LOLBin Usage: net1
rules.description: Detects usage of Living off the Land binary net1.

rules.title: Windows LOLBin Usage: powershell
rules.description: Detects usage of Living off the Land binary powershell.

rules.title: Windows LOLBin Usage: regsvr32
rules.description: Detects usage of Living off the Land binary regsvr32.

rules.title: Windows LOLBin Usage: rundll32
rules.description: Detects usage of Living off the Land binary rundll32.

rules.title: Windows LOLBin Usage: sc
rules.description: Detects usage of Living off the Land binary sc.

rules.title: Windows LOLBin Usage: schtasks
rules.description: Detects usage of Living off the Land binary schtasks.

rules.title: Windows LOLBin Usage: systeminfo
rules.description: Detects usage of Living off the Land binary systeminfo.

rules.title: Windows LOLBin Usage: taskkill
rules.description: Detects usage of Living off the Land binary taskkill.

rules.title: Windows LOLBin Usage: tasklist
rules.description: Detects usage of Living off the Land binary tasklist.

rules.title: Windows LOLBin Usage: whoami
rules.description: Detects usage of Living off the Land binary whoami.

rules.title: Windows LOLBin Usage: wmic
rules.description: Detects usage of Living off the Land binary wmic.

rules.title: Windows LOLBin Usage: wscript
rules.description: Detects usage of Living off the Land binary wscript.

rules.title: Windows LSASS Memory Access
rules.description: Detects tools accessing LSASS process memory for credential dumping. LSASS stores authentication credentials and is a primary target for credential theft.

rules.title: Windows Management Instrumentation Event Subscription
rules.description: Detects WMI event subscription creation for persistence via command-line WMI tools.

rules.title: Windows Mimikatz Execution Patterns
rules.description: Detects execution of Mimikatz or tools with Mimikatz-like command line patterns used for credential harvesting from Windows systems.

rules.title: Windows MSBuild Execution for Code Bypass
rules.description: Detects execution of MSBuild.exe which can compile and execute inline C# code from XML project files, bypassing application whitelisting and AppLocker policies.

rules.title: Windows Network Share Discovery
rules.description: Detects commands used to enumerate network shares. Attackers discover shared resources to identify sensitive data repositories, admin shares, and lateral movement paths.

rules.title: Windows NTDS.dit Database Extraction
rules.description: Detects attempts to extract the Active Directory NTDS.dit database file using ntdsutil, vssadmin shadow copies, or direct copy commands. This file contains all domain password hashes.

rules.title: Windows Pass-the-Hash Attack Indicators
rules.description: Detects command-line patterns associated with pass-the-hash attacks using tools like Impacket, CrackMapExec, or built-in Windows commands with NTLM hash parameters.

rules.title: Windows PowerShell Download Cradle
rules.description: Detects PowerShell download cradle patterns commonly used to fetch and execute remote payloads including Invoke-WebRequest, Net.WebClient, and Start-BitsTransfer.

rules.title: Windows Process Hollowing Indicators
rules.description: Detects suspicious process creation patterns indicative of process hollowing where a legitimate process is spawned in a suspended state and its memory replaced with malicious code.

rules.title: Windows PsExec Remote Execution
rules.description: Detects use of PsExec or similar Sysinternals tools for remote command execution, commonly used for lateral movement in compromised networks.

rules.title: Windows RDP Lateral Movement
rules.description: Detects use of mstsc.exe (Remote Desktop client) or enabling RDP via registry, indicating potential lateral movement between systems.

rules.title: Windows Registry Run Key Modification
rules.description: Detects modification of Windows Registry Run/RunOnce keys for persistence, allowing malicious programs to execute at system startup or user login.

rules.title: Windows Remote Access Tool Detected
rules.description: Detects installation or execution of remote access tools commonly abused by attackers. Tools like AnyDesk, TeamViewer, ngrok, and ScreenConnect provide persistent remote access that can bypass firewall rules.

rules.title: Windows Remote Management Shell via Winrs
rules.description: Detects usage of winrs.exe for remote command execution over WinRM.

rules.title: Windows Remote System Discovery
rules.description: Detects commands and tools used to discover remote systems on the network. Attackers perform network sweeps, ARP scans, and DNS lookups to identify targets for lateral movement.

rules.title: Windows Scheduled Task Creation via Schtasks
rules.description: Detects creation of scheduled tasks via schtasks.exe with suspicious patterns. Scheduled tasks are a common persistence and execution mechanism.

rules.title: Windows Screen Capture Activity
rules.description: Detects screen capture tools and commands used by attackers to collect visual intelligence from victim systems. Screen captures can reveal sensitive data displayed on screen, credentials in open windows, and security posture.

rules.title: Windows Script Host Execution from Temp
rules.description: Detects cscript or wscript executing scripts from temporary directories.

rules.title: Windows Service Created with Suspicious Binary Path
rules.description: Detects creation of Windows services pointing to executables in user-writable paths.

rules.title: Windows Service Creation via SC
rules.description: Detects creation of new Windows services using sc.exe, which may indicate persistence or privilege escalation.

rules.title: Windows Shadow Copy Deletion
rules.description: Detects deletion of volume shadow copies via vssadmin or wmic, a common ransomware indicator to prevent recovery from encrypted files.

rules.title: Windows Spearphishing Attachment Execution
rules.description: Detects suspicious child processes spawned by Microsoft Office applications, indicating potential macro-based spearphishing payload execution. Office documents should not spawn cmd.exe, powershell.exe, or scripting engines.

rules.title: Windows UAC Bypass Attempt
rules.description: Detects common UAC bypass techniques including fodhelper, eventvwr, and sdclt abuse. These auto-elevate binaries can be hijacked via registry to execute arbitrary code with elevated privileges.

rules.title: Windows Vault Enumeration
rules.description: Detects enumeration of Windows Vault credentials using cmdkey or rundll32 with keymgr.dll.

rules.title: Windows WDigest Authentication Enabled for Credential Harvesting
rules.description: Detects enabling WDigest authentication via registry modification. When enabled, plaintext credentials are stored in LSASS memory, making them available for extraction by tools like Mimikatz.

rules.title: Windows WMI Event Subscription Persistence
rules.description: Detects creation of WMI event subscriptions for persistence, allowing attackers to execute code triggered by system events.

rules.title: Winlogon Helper DLL Modification
rules.description: Detects modification of Winlogon helper DLL registry entries for persistence.

rules.title: WinRM Lateral Movement via PowerShell
rules.description: Detects WinRM-based lateral movement using PowerShell Invoke-Command or Enter-PSSession.

rules.title: WMI Event Subscription Persistence
rules.description: Detects creation of WMI event subscriptions for fileless persistence.

rules.title: WMI Process Execution via Wmic
rules.description: Detects WMI-based process execution using wmic process call create.

rules.title: Wscript Running Encoded Script
rules.description: Detects execution of encoded VBScript or JScript files (.vbe, .jse) via wscript.

rules.title: XSL Script Processing via WMIC or Msxsl
rules.description: Detects execution of XSL scripts through WMIC /format or msxsl.exe for code execution.

rules.title: Detect Suspicious Commands Initiated by Webserver Processes
rules.description:
This query was originally published in the threat analytics report, Operation Soft Cell.
Operation Soft Cell is a series of campaigns targeting users' call logs at telecommunications providers throughout the world. These attacks date from as early as 2012.
Operation Soft Cell operators sometimes use legitimate web server processes to launch commands, especially for network discovery and user/owner discovery. The following query detects activity of this kind.
Reference - https://www.cybereason.com/blog/operation-soft-cell-a-worldwide-campaign-against-telecommunications-providers

rules.title: Discord download invoked from cmd line
rules.description: 'This hunting query looks for hosts that have attempted to interact with the Discord CDN. This activity is not normally invoked from the command line and could indicate C2, exfiltration, or malware delivery activity.'

rules.title: Doppelpaymer Stop Services
rules.description:
This query was originally published in the threat analytics report, Doppelpaymer: More human-operated ransomware. There is also a related blog.
DoppelPaymer is ransomware that is spread manually by human operators. These operators have exhibited extensive knowledge of system administration and common network security misconfigurations. They often use stolen credentials from over-privileged service accounts to turn off security software, run malicious commands, and spread malware throughout an organization.
The following query detects attempts to stop security services.
The See also section below lists links to other queries associated with DoppelPaymer.
References:
https://msrc-blog.microsoft.com/2019/11/20/customer-guidance-for-the-dopplepaymer-ransomware/
https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/wdsi/threats/malware-encyclopedia-description?Name=Ransom:Win32/DoppelPaymer.KM!MTB

rules.title: DopplePaymer Procdump
rules.description:
This query was originally published in the threat analytics report, Doppelpaymer: More human-operated ransomware. There is also a related blog.
DoppelPaymer is ransomware that is spread manually by human operators. These operators have exhibited extensive knowledge of system administration and common network security misconfigurations. For example, they use SysInternal utilities such as ProcDump to dump credentials from LSASS. They often use these stolen credentials to turn off security software, run malicious commands, and spread malware throughout an organization.
The following query detects ProcDump being used to dump credentials from LSASS.
The See also section below lists links to other queries associated with DoppelPaymer.
References:
https://msrc-blog.microsoft.com/2019/11/20/customer-guidance-for-the-dopplepaymer-ransomware/
https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/wdsi/threats/malware-encyclopedia-description?Name=Ransom:Win32/DoppelPaymer.KM!MTB
https://docs.microsoft.com/sysinternals/downloads/procdump
https://docs.microsoft.com/windows-server/security/credentials-protection-and-management/configuring-additional-lsa-protection

rules.title: LemonDuck-component-names
rules.description: LemonDuck is an actively updated and robust malware primarily known for its botnet and cryptocurrency mining objectives. First discovered in 2019, LemonDuck has since adopted more sophisticated behavior and escalated its operations in 2021. Today, beyond using resources for its traditional bot and mining activities, LemonDuck steals credentials, removes security controls, spreads via emails, moves laterally, and ultimately drops more tools for human-operated activity.

rules.title: LSASS Credential Dumping with Procdump
rules.description:
This query was originally published in the threat analytics report, "Exchange Server zero-days exploited in the wild".
In early March 2021, Microsoft released patches for four different zero-day vulnerabilities affecting Microsoft Exchange Server. The vulnerabilities were being used in a coordinated attack. For more information on the vulnerabilities, visit the following links:
1. CVE-2021-26855
2. CVE-2021-26857
3. CVE-2021-26858
4. CVE-2021-27065
The following query looks for evidence of Procdump being used to dump credentials from LSASS, the Local Security Authentication Server. This might indicate an attacker has compromised user accounts.
More queries related to this threat can be found under the See also section of this page.
Reference - https://msrc-blog.microsoft.com/2021/03/02/multiple-security-updates-released-for-exchange-server/

rules.title: NRT Base64 Encoded Windows Process Command-lines
rules.description: 'This detection identifies instances of a base64 encoded PE file header seen in the process command line parameter.'

rules.title: Office Apps Launching Wscipt
rules.description:
This query was originally published in the threat analytics report, Trickbot: Pervasive & underestimated.
Trickbot is a very prevalent piece of malware with an array of malicious capabilities. Originally designed to steal banking credentials, it has since evolved into a modular trojan that can deploy other malware, disable security software, and perform command-and-control (C2) operations.
Trickbot is frequently spread through email. An attacker will send a target a message with an attachment containing a malicious macro. If the target enables the macro, it will write a JScript Encoded (JSE) file to disk (JScript is a Microsoft dialect of ECMAScript). The JSE file will then be launched using wscript.exe to perform a variety of malicious tasks, particularly reconnaissance.
The following query detects when Office applications have launched wscript.exe to run a JSE file.
See Detect rundll.exe being used for reconnaissance and command-and-control for another query related to Trickbot activity.
Reference - https://attack.mitre.org/software/S0266/

app_queries.title: APM: Browser Attack to WAF Block Correlation
app_queries.description: Summary table joining browser-side attack telemetry from SOC Application Logs with the WAF signal that flagged the same Trace ID. Hosts a count of how many traces fired both an APM detection and a WAF block, so the SOC can prove cross-tier defence-in-depth coverage.

app_queries.title: APM: Browser Fingerprinting via Canvas/WebGL/AudioContext
app_queries.description: Detects browser fingerprinting techniques using Canvas API, WebGL parameters, AudioContext, and other APIs commonly used to uniquely identify browsers. Excessive fingerprinting API calls indicate tracking scripts or reconnaissance.

app_queries.title: APM: Clickjacking - Missing Frame Protection Headers
app_queries.description: Detects HTTP responses missing X-Frame-Options or Content-Security-Policy frame-ancestors directives, indicating vulnerability to clickjacking attacks. Monitors APM response headers for missing security controls.

app_queries.title: APM: CSRF Token Missing or Invalid on State-Changing Request
app_queries.description: Detects HTTP POST/PUT/DELETE requests to state-changing endpoints that lack CSRF tokens or have invalid tokens, indicating potential Cross-Site Request Forgery. Uses APM span attributes to detect missing security headers.

app_queries.title: APM: DOM-Based Attack via Dangerous JavaScript APIs
app_queries.description: Detects DOM-based attacks using dangerous JavaScript APIs observed in browser telemetry spans. Monitors for document.cookie access, eval() calls, innerHTML manipulation, and location/hash-based injection in OpenTelemetry browser spans.

app_queries.title: APM: Octo Demo API Gateway Edge Decisions
app_queries.description: API Gateway edge-policy, quota, auth, and backend decisions captured by the Octo attack lab and correlated to the same Attack ID, Request ID, and Trace ID used by APM spans and application logs.

app_queries.title: APM: Octo Demo Attack Path Link
app_queries.description: Link analysis for the Octo attack-lab path. Groups attack stages, traces, and spans into a single investigation canvas with custom tiles for the number of attacks, events, traces, and stages.

app_queries.title: APM: Octo Demo Attack Timeline
app_queries.description: Chronological attack-lab events from the Octo APM demo, keyed by Attack ID, Run ID, Request ID, Trace ID, and Span ID so operators can pivot from security logs into APM spans.

app_queries.title: APM: Octo Demo Attack Trace Correlation
app_queries.description: Trace-level rollup of attack-lab evidence. Shows which Attack ID and Run ID have spans, MITRE stages, payment redirect evidence, and compromised VM evidence on the same Trace ID.

app_queries.title: APM: Octo Demo Compromised VM Pivots
app_queries.description: Compromised VM and process evidence from the Octo attack lab, including OSQuery findings, instance OCIDs, host roles, and process command lines.

app_queries.title: APM: Octo Demo Database Spans
app_queries.description: Database-related APM spans for octo-apm-demo, grouped by target, span name, slow request flag, and error type.

app_queries.title: APM: Octo Demo Error Logs by Span
app_queries.description: Error and warning logs for octo-apm-demo grouped by severity, error type, span name, and trace. Use this to jump from a failed log line to the span that produced it.

app_queries.title: APM: Octo Demo Java Sidecar Error Correlation
app_queries.description: Java APM sidecar and payment-gateway error evidence correlated with Octo checkout and attack-lab traces. Highlights the Java path, error type, status code, and workflow step that should be investigated in APM Trace Explorer.

app_queries.title: APM: Octo Demo Metric Samples
app_queries.description: Metric sample inventory for octo-apm-demo. Displays metric names, values, units, and routes as parsed from SOC Application Logs.

app_queries.title: APM: Octo Demo OSQuery Host Evidence
app_queries.description: Host-level OSQuery findings emitted by the Octo attack lab, grouped by compromised host, instance OCID, query name, and severity so investigators can pivot from APM trace context to VM evidence.

app_queries.title: APM: Octo Demo Payment Threats
app_queries.description: Payment interception and redirect signals emitted by the Octo attack lab. Keeps only demo-safe payment metadata such as provider, status, brand, last4, risk score, and redirect URL.

app_queries.title: APM: Octo Demo RED Metrics
app_queries.description: Request, error, and duration-oriented telemetry for the Octo APM demo application. Groups metric samples by service, route, and response code so operators can find request volume, error concentration, and slow-path symptoms.

app_queries.title: APM: Octo Demo Request and Error Timeline
app_queries.description: Timeline of octo-apm-demo application events by severity, suitable for spotting bursts of warnings, errors, and normal request flow.

app_queries.title: APM: Octo Demo API Gateway Threat Detection Rule
app_queries.description: Scheduled-rule-safe count of Octo API Gateway threat decisions tied to an attack ID and trace.

app_queries.title: APM: Octo Demo Compromised VM Detection Rule
app_queries.description: Scheduled-rule-safe count of compromised VM evidence correlated to Octo attack IDs.

app_queries.title: APM: Octo Demo Java Payment Error Detection Rule

app_queries.title: APM: Octo Demo Payment Interception Detection Rule
